metadata.title: ChatGPT-5.5 or Claude 4.7?
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: this is Liam, in for Bear. Everyone wants one AI. One tab, one invoice, one clean answer they can give their boss. Claude was the default recommendation for months. Then ChatGPT had a week in April. Best images ever. Better search, coding, Google Sheets integration. Three days. Then new evidence changed the stack. This is the honest version of which AI for what.

props.command: which AI should I actually be using right now?

beats.narration_text: The one rule, before anything else: if your company pays for Claude Enterprise, use Claude properly. If your company pays for ChatGPT Enterprise, use ChatGPT properly. If you can afford both, pay for both. That's not a dodge — it's the honest answer. The real benchmark isn't a chart online. The real benchmark is you testing the model on your actual work. Benchmarks measure what labs want measured.

artifactLines: Company pays for Claude Enterprise → use Claude properly.
Company pays for ChatGPT Enterprise → use ChatGPT properly.
Can afford both → pay for both.
Don't write in English → Gemini often wins on benchmarks.
Real benchmark: test on your actual work, not someone's chart.

beats.narration_text: Claude works well as a default work brain for a boring reason: persistent context. A Cowork folder can hold about-me, my-company, and Skills files. Claude reads them at the start of a session, so the user does not have to repeat context. One prompt does it: 'I want to do X for Y. Read my folder and use AskUserQuestion first.' That's probably better than a hundred magic prompts you've saved and never reused. Context is the whole game.

props.title: claude-context-prompt.md
props.code:
I want to do [TASK] for [SUCCESS CRITERIA]. But first, read my folder and use AskUserQuestion. # What AskUserQuestion does:
# Claude prompts YOU instead of guessing.
# You click answers. Claude gets the context it needs.
# Result: better output, fewer revisions.

beats.narration_text: Then that April week happened. ChatGPT released materially stronger image generation. Not just stylistically — functionally. Edit in context, compose accurately, follow complex prompts. Then Google Sheets integration. Then improved search. Three days of releases that genuinely moved the stack. That makes ChatGPT useful again for a specific slice of the workflow.

shot.slotNote: SLOT — fill media/B03.png (screenshot of a ChatGPT image generation example from the article period, or the task-specific AI stack comparison graphic)

beats.narration_text: The updated stack, honest and specific. Claude for work — writing, contracts, briefs, analysis, long documents. ChatGPT for images, search when depth matters, and Google Sheets when you need the integration. Gemini for non-English work where it still outperforms on benchmarks. Gamma for presentations. Not a forever stack — In practice, that plainly. But it's the stack right now, built on what each tool actually does better.

artifactLines: Claude Cowork → writing, analysis, long docs, contracts.
Not forever. The market ships every week.

beats.narration_text: Two shortcuts for the people who want the decision made for them. If most of your job is writing — contracts, briefs, copy, memos, anything long-form — Claude Cowork is still the answer. The folder context, the Connectors, the multi-agent Cowork sessions: nothing else runs that workflow as cleanly. If you care about images, search, or Google Sheets integration, the new ChatGPT is back at the top. Those are the two exits. Pick one.

props.artifactHeading: Stop reading here if one fits
artifactLines: Most of your job is writing → Claude Cowork, still king.
You need images, search, or Google Sheets → new ChatGPT.
Both matter to you → pay for both, use each for its lane.
Still unsure → test both on your actual work this week.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Test this in thirty minutes, not thirty days. One real task in Claude, one real task in ChatGPT. Same task. Compare the output. Then you'll know which one earned the tab.

props.command: I want to test Claude vs ChatGPT on my actual work. Take the task I describe, complete it fully, then tell me what context or file you'd need to do this consistently — so I can build a setup that runs this every week.

props.title: ChatGPT-5.5 or Claude 4.7?
props.subline: right tool · right task · test it
